title: lavandula stoechas subsp. pedunculata
intro: Lavandula stoechas subsp. pedunculata, also known as the 'Penduncular Dalmatian Lavender', is a subspecies of the Dalmatian Lavender plant, characterized by its unique, drooping flowers and a woody stem. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its calming and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to its essential oil content. The health benefits of Lavandula stoechas subsp. pedunculata include reducing stress and anxiety, promoting better sleep, and alleviating symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and mood swings. Lavandula stoechas subsp. pedunculata can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, to harness its medicinal properties.
